page: Microsoft Azure D4ls_v7 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine belonging to the Dlsv7 server family. It is powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor operating at 4.2 GHz, configured with 2 physical cores and 2 threads per core, yielding 4 vCPUs. The processor features a 480 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. It supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for vector and matrix operations. The instance is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB per vCPU, and does not include local storage or bundled GPUs.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-threaded performance, with single-threaded gzip compression reaching 34.6 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression achieving a top-tier result of 14.78 GB/sec. However, multi-threaded performance is relatively weak, as shown by a stress-ng multi-core score of 4513.8 and a multi-threaded bzip3 decompression rate of 28.69 GB/sec. Memory read bandwidth is average, measuring 335.78 GB/sec for small cached blocks and 45.33 GB/sec for large uncached blocks, while write bandwidth for large blocks is weak at 23.19 GB/sec. Passmark CPU and memory scores sit in the average tier, with a CPU score of 8626.16 and a database operations score of 2650.49.

The D4ls_v7 offers a cost-efficient profile for compute-bound workloads that do not require large memory footprints or local storage. Its dedicated CPU allocation and high clock speed deliver reliable single-core execution, but the 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU and lack of local NVMe storage present clear tradeoffs for memory-intensive or high-I/O applications. It is capable of handling moderate workloads such as static web serving, Redis operations, and lightweight single-threaded data processing, but is poorly suited for larger LLM inference or heavy multi-threaded database tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure D4ls_v7 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the Dlsv7 family, powered by a dedicated 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with 4 vCPUs (2 physical cores) and 8.0 GB of RAM. It features a large 480 MB L3 cache and supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets. The instance does not include local storage or GPUs. Benchmarks show strong single-threaded performance, highlighted by top-tier bzip3 decompression and strong gzip compression, though multi-threaded scaling and large-block memory write speeds are weak. With 2.0 GB of memory per vCPU and no local storage, this instance offers a cost-efficient option for compute-focused workloads with low memory requirements. It is best suited for static web serving, lightweight single-threaded processing, and basic Redis operations.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D4ls_v7 is a 4-vCPU virtual machine featuring a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor, 8.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage, offering strong single-threaded performance for compute-focused workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D4ls_v7 is a 4-vCPU virtual machine with a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon CPU and 8.0 GB of RAM, optimized for low-memory compute workloads.
tagline: A low-memory, compute-focused virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processing and strong single-threaded performance for lightweight workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with 4 vCPUs
8.0 GB of system memory (2.0 GB per vCPU)
Strong single-threaded compression and decompression performance
No bundled local storage or GPU resources
Best suited for static web serving and lightweight single-threaded tasks
